articles.title: Heatwave in Odisha: Several districts cross 40°C, Jharsuguda hottest; IMD issues warning - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Unbearable heatwave conditions continue to grip Odisha, with temperatures soaring across large parts of the state, particularly in the western region. Several districts have recorded temperatures above 40°C, indicating a sharp rise in heat intensity.
Jharsuguda emerged as the hottest place in the state, recording a maximum temperature of 40.6°C. Titlagarh also touched 40°C, while Hirakud and Sonepur registered 39.2°C each.
Angul recorded 39.1°C, and Bhadrak and Bolangir reported temperatures of 39°C. In most other cities, the mercury remained above 38°C, reflecting widespread heatwave conditions.
Western Odisha Bears the Brunt
Districts including Nuapada, Titlagarh, Bolangir, Sonepur, Boudh, Kalahandi, Jharsuguda, Sundargarh and Rourkela are expected to face extreme weather conditions, with temperatures likely to hover between 40°C and 42°C.
Hot and humid conditions continue to prevail across interior Odisha, intensifying discomfort for residents.
In contrast, coastal regions are experiencing relatively cooler weather, leading to noticeable variations in temperature across the state.
The prevailing weather pattern has created a clear divide, with inland districts witnessing rising heat levels while coastal belts remain comparatively less affected.
Heat Warning Issued, Conditions Likely to Persist
The Meteorological Department has issued a ‘hot and humid’ warning for Odisha, stating that such conditions are likely to persist over the next three days.
A Yellow warning for heat conditions has also been issued for Ganjam, Gajapati and Puri districts.
While thunderstorms and rainfall have been forecast in some areas, the primary concern remains the intensifying heat.
Weather conditions are expected to change from April 5 onwards, with possible rain, lightning, and wind speeds of 30 to 40 kmph in select districts.
